title: Previous client wants re-entry
short_description: Route directly to admissions. No supervisor call needed.
do_now: Direct the person to admissions.
documentation: Optional log / handoff note if needed
flags: No call required except admissions handoff

title: Positive drug screen other than prescribed medication
short_description: Positive screen triggers property restriction, supervisor call, and chart documentation.
do_now: Place client on property restriction until treatment team follow-up.
Call shift supervisor or supervisor on-call to speak with the client.
Email all staff with results and information.

title: Client reports after curfew / late from pass
short_description: Assess, UDS, document, and email. No call unless behavior escalates.
do_now: Conduct visual assessment for suspected substance use.
Complete a UDS.
If client refuses UDS, record refusal as automatic positive result.
follow_up: Escalate only if client becomes combative or confrontational.
special_notes: No call required unless behavior escalates.

title: Self-injurious behavior
short_description: Self-harm behavior requires universal precautions, injury assessment, and clinical on-call.
do_now: Use universal precautions and put gloves on first.
Secure the tool used if safe to do so.
Immediately assess the injury.
Call clinical on-call.
documentation: CIR if incident threshold is met
follow_up: Use first aid skills and on-call staff input to decide on higher level of care.
If injury is severe, move to medical emergency / 911 workflow.
special_notes: Clinical on-call assesses whether higher level of care is needed.

title: Inclement weather
short_description: Follow site-specific weather instructions and keep clients inside when conditions are dangerous.
do_now: Read and follow the inclement weather policy and current emailed instructions.
If dangerous weather is present, do not allow clients outside.
documentation: Shift report if operational changes are made
special_notes: Site-specific instructions may vary by event.

title: Internal abuse allegation
short_description: Call RST Manager; determine APS/CPS need; keep confidential.
do_now: Call RST Manager.
Limit sharing to need-to-know staff only.
Preserve facts and immediate safety.
follow_up: Leadership determines whether APS or CPS report is warranted and what further action is taken.
special_notes: All information should be kept in strict confidence.

title: Inappropriate or unprofessional display from coworker (no physical harm)
short_description: Route to direct supervisor by email. No crisis call required.
do_now: Email the direct supervisor with factual information.
special_notes: No call required when there is no physical harm.

title: Unsafe outside conditions / active shooting / dangerous neighborhood conditions
short_description: External danger. No clients outside until conditions are safe.
do_now: Keep all clients inside until it is deemed safe.
If active shooter or immediate external danger exists, call 911.
Follow site lockdown / shelter instructions as applicable.
follow_up: Leadership notification required when first responders are called.
special_notes: This includes dangerous weather and external violence threats.

title: Malfunctioning appliance / equipment
short_description: Email supervisor / manager; call maintenance for critical operational failures.
do_now: Email the shift supervisor and RST Manager about the malfunction.
If the malfunction is critical for operations, call on-call maintenance.
If provisions or relocation decisions are needed, then call shift supervisor or on-call supervisor.
follow_up: Verify the correct maintenance number in admin before production use.
special_notes: Critical examples in source: no heat or no water.

title: Media involvement
short_description: Only executive leadership speaks to media.
do_now: Do not provide comments to media.
Call the Program Manager and inform them media is asking questions.
special_notes: Only executive leadership may speak with media about agency matters.

title: Police intervention for order / therapeutic environment
short_description: Use police as last resort. Non-emergency uses LMPD non-emergency; emergency uses 911.
do_now: For non-emergency situations, call LMPD non-emergency.
For emergency situations, call 911.
Consult on-call staff to inform them police were called.
special_notes: Police should never be Plan A or Plan B; use when safety is compromised and other interventions are not enough.

title: No staffing
short_description: Operational emergency. Call supervisor on-call immediately.
do_now: Call supervisor on-call immediately.

title: Vehicle work accident with client or alone
short_description: Call police for every work accident; call 911 if anyone is hurt.
do_now: Call police even if there is no visible damage.
If anyone is hurt, call 911.
File a police report.
Report the incident to the Program Manager.
follow_up: Call direct supervisor. If only staff involved, call immediate supervisor.
special_notes: This applies whether a client is in the vehicle or staff is alone.
